annotations.label: Isomorphism of probability spaces

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism between two probability spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has MeasurePreserving and MeasurableEquiv (≃ᵐ) whose combination is exactly a probability-space isomorphism, but there is no single packaged definition.

annotations.label: Isomorphic probability spaces

anchor.snippet: isomorphic if there exists an isomorphism between them

annotations.note: Existence of a measure-preserving MeasurableEquiv expresses this, but Mathlib has no named 'isomorphic probability spaces' relation.

annotations.label: Isomorphism modulo zero

anchor.snippet: if there exist null sets

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of isomorphism mod 0 (isomorphism after deleting null sets) of measure spaces.

annotations.label: Standard probability space

anchor.snippet: A probability space is standard

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes StandardBorelSpace (the measurable structure) but not the measure-theoretic Lebesgue–Rokhlin standard probability space defined via mod-0 isomorphism to an interval plus atoms.

annotations.label: A naive white noise

anchor.snippet: may be thought of as the product

annotations.note: This pathological product-of-continuum-many-copies example is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: A perforated interval

anchor.snippet: be a set whose inner Lebesgue measure is equal to 0

annotations.note: This counterexample using a set of inner Lebesgue measure 0 is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: A superfluous measurable set

anchor.snippet: be as in the previous example

annotations.note: This non-standardness counterexample is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pushforward measure

anchor.snippet: induces a pushforward measure

annotations.note: Measure.map is exactly the pushforward of a measure along a measurable function.

annotations.label: Generating function

anchor.snippet: is called generating if

annotations.note: Mathlib has MeasurableSpace.comap/generateFrom but no 'generating (mod 0)' predicate for a random variable on a probability space.

annotations.label: Insufficient condition for generating

anchor.snippet: The following condition is not sufficient

annotations.note: This counterexample about a non-sufficient condition for generating is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standardness criterion (single random variable)

anchor.snippet: Let a measurable function

annotations.note: Rokhlin's standardness criterion via a generating random variable is not formalized.

annotations.label: Standardness criterion (random vector)

anchor.snippet: The same theorem holds for any

annotations.note: The standardness criterion for a generating random vector is not formalized.

annotations.label: Standardness criterion (random sequence)

anchor.snippet: The theorem still holds for the space

annotations.note: The standardness criterion for a generating random sequence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generating for a sequence of events

anchor.snippet: if the random variables

annotations.note: The equivalence between generating events and generating indicator variables is not formalized.

annotations.label: Basis of a probability space

anchor.snippet: are called bases of the probability space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a 'basis' (generating sequence of events) of a probability space.

annotations.label: Complete mod 0 basis

anchor.snippet: A basis is called complete mod 0

annotations.note: The 'complete mod 0' property of a basis is not formalized.

annotations.label: Completeness independent of basis (Rokhlin)

anchor.snippet: Rokhlin proved that if a probability space is complete mod 0

annotations.note: Rokhlin's theorem that completeness mod 0 is independent of the chosen basis is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence of the four cases

anchor.snippet: The four cases treated above are mutually equivalent

annotations.note: The mutual equivalence of the single-variable, vector, sequence, and events formulations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Countably separated

anchor.snippet: we get the property well known as being countably separated

annotations.note: CountablySeparated states points are separated by a countable family of measurable sets, matching the definition.

annotations.label: Countably generated

anchor.snippet: we get the property well known as being countably generated

annotations.note: CountablyGenerated states the σ-algebra is generated by a countable family of sets, matching the definition.

annotations.label: Injective implies generating

anchor.snippet: Every injective measurable function from a standard probability space

annotations.note: Mathlib proves an injective measurable map from a standard Borel space is a measurable embedding, but does not phrase this as 'generating mod 0' for probability spaces.

annotations.label: Countably generated is mod 0 invariant

anchor.snippet: is invariant under mod 0 isomorphisms

annotations.note: Mod-0 isomorphism is absent in Mathlib, so the invariance of countable generation under it is not formalized.

annotations.label: Countably separated iff cardinality ≤ continuum

anchor.snippet: is countably separated if and only if the cardinality

annotations.note: Mathlib gives a measurable injection of a countably separated space into ℕ→Bool (yielding cardinality ≤ continuum), but the full iff with cardinality is not stated.

annotations.label: Standardness via absolute measurability

anchor.snippet: is standard if it is countably separated, countably generated, and absolutely measurable

annotations.note: Absolute measurability and this equivalent definition of standardness are not formalized.

annotations.label: Standardness via perfectness

anchor.snippet: is standard if it is countably separated and perfect

annotations.note: Perfect measures and this characterization of standardness are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standardness via topology

anchor.snippet: is standard if there exists a topology

annotations.note: StandardBorelSpace is defined exactly as 'there exists a Polish topology whose Borel σ-algebra is the given one', matching the topological core but without the measure-completion part.

annotations.label: Distributions on the real line are standard

anchor.snippet: Every probability distribution on the space

annotations.note: ℝ is a StandardBorelSpace (Polish), giving the Borel-structure half, but standardness of the probability space itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Distributions on Polish spaces are standard

anchor.snippet: The same holds on every Polish space

annotations.note: standardBorel_of_polish shows every Polish space is standard Borel, but the measure-theoretic standardness of distributions on it is not formalized.

annotations.label: Wiener measure gives a standard space

anchor.snippet: the Wiener measure turns the Polish space

annotations.note: Wiener measure / Brownian motion is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Joint distribution of a sequence

anchor.snippet: for every sequence of random variables, their joint distribution turns the Polish space

annotations.note: ℝ^∞ is standard Borel via the pi instance, but standardness of the joint-distribution probability space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Product of two standard spaces

anchor.snippet: The product of two standard probability spaces

annotations.note: StandardBorelSpace.prod gives closure of standard Borel spaces under binary products; the probability-space version is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Product of countably many standard spaces

anchor.snippet: The same holds for the product of countably many spaces

annotations.note: StandardBorelSpace.pi gives closure under countable products of standard Borel spaces; the probability-space statement is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Measurable subset of a standard space

anchor.snippet: A measurable subset of a standard probability space

annotations.note: MeasurableSet.standardBorel shows a measurable subset of a standard Borel space is standard Borel; the probability-space version is not formalized.

annotations.label: Measure on a standard Borel space

anchor.snippet: Every probability measure on a standard Borel space

annotations.note: StandardBorelSpace is formalized but the statement that any probability measure on it yields a standard probability space is not, since standard probability space is absent.

annotations.label: Existence of regular conditional probabilities

anchor.snippet: this is possible, and well known as canonical system of measures

annotations.note: Measure.condKernel together with its IsCondKernel instance gives disintegration ρ = ρ.fst ⊗ₘ ρ.condKernel for standard Borel target spaces, i.e. existence of regular conditional probabilities.

annotations.label: Measure preserving map covers standard space

anchor.snippet: if the probability spaces

annotations.note: MeasurePreserving is formalized, but the standard-space-specific theorem about essential surjectivity/coverage is not.

annotations.label: Inverse of one-to-one measure preserving map

anchor.snippet: is also one-to-one then every

annotations.note: MeasurePreserving.symm gives that the inverse of a measure-preserving MeasurableEquiv is measure preserving, but the standard-space result that an injective measure-preserving map automatically has measurable inverse is not packaged.

annotations.label: Measure algebra

anchor.snippet: form a normed complete Boolean algebra called the measure algebra

annotations.note: The measure algebra (Boolean algebra of measurable sets mod null sets with its metric/norm) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homomorphisms of measure algebras come from maps

anchor.snippet: for standard probability spaces each

annotations.note: The result that every measure-algebra homomorphism between standard spaces is induced by a measure-preserving map is not formalized.
